Citizens.” More information Worldwide Caution: The Department of State advises Americans worldwide to exercise increased caution. Do Not Travel to these areas due to terrorism and crime: Guayaquil, south of Portete de Tarqui Avenue. Reconsider Travel to these areas due to terrorism and crime: Guayaquil north of Portete de Tarqui Avenue. Advisory summary Crime and kidnapping Crime is a widespread problem in Ecuador. Violent crime typically associated with narcotrafficking is common. Areas with transnational criminal organizations see much higher rates of violent crime. citizens and LPRs have been victims of kidnapping and extortion. Terrorism There is risk of terrorist violence, including terrorist attacks and other activity in Ecuador.

source_name: UK FCDO Travel Advice — Safety and Security
excerpt: Safety and security - Ecuador travel advice - GOV.UK Cookies on GOV.UK We use some essential cookies to make this website work. Warning FCDO advises against all but essential travel to parts of Ecuador. Subscriptions Get email alerts Safety and security You should also read FCDO ’s overall travel advice and regional risks advice . Find out how to reduce your risk from terrorism while abroad . Street demonstrations, protests and strikes are common and can occur without warning. Crime Protecting yourself and your belongings Mugging and pickpocketing are common, take care in Quito’s old town and at transport hubs. Violent crime and armed robbery On 14 October 2025, a car bomb exploded near the Mall del Sol shopping centre and the Sheraton Hotel in northern Guayaquil, resulting in multiple injuries and at least one fatality. Crimes with the threat of violence, such as gunpoint robbery and home invasion, can happen throughout Ecuador. Most violent crime is gang-related, but tourists can be affected. The murder rate in Guayaquil is very high, but is mainly gang-related. Since March 2023, organised crime gangs have caused small explosions and made false bomb threats in Quito and Guayaquil
